title: After-Action Report — Fresh-League 500 (`derive_linear_weights` KeyError) + Wiring Salary / Manager / Sellback Cards
scope: Two threads landed on top of each other in the same session: 1. Audit the app for unwired features ("data exists, user can't see it" gaps). 2. The user reported `i can't get a new league to load with the latest files on main` — fresh league created post-restart was 500'ing on every page that touched batter / pitcher stats.

rationale.item: The audit surfaced three template-level gaps that needed surfacing; the deploy bug was a shared analytics helper that crashed when there was no game data to derive linear weights from.

Root cause of the 500: derive_linear_weights() builds the rv dict from events observed in game_pa_log; with zero games played the comprehension produces only keys it later adds unconditionally, and the wOBA-weight block dereferences unguarded keys (rv['1B'] etc.) → KeyError: '1B'.
Sellback trades were persisted by the trade mechanic (PR #35) and rendered in the live-auction replay, but a user who skipped the live replay never learned that any sellbacks had happened.

actions.description: Fix: defensively populate every event type the rest of derive_linear_weights dereferences, defaulting to 0.0 (rv.setdefault for out/1B/2B/3B/HR; BB via _walk_run_value, HBP = BB)

Salaries on player detail + team roster: 'Salary' line in the player detail header strip; 'Salary' column on batter and pitcher roster tables with data-sort-value; rendered through the existing money Jinja filter

Manager archetype + tactical axes on /team/<id>: compact 'Manager' strip showing the archetype's friendly label plus the eight axes as integer percentages with hover-titles; new archetype_label Jinja filter wrapping o27v2.managers.archetype_label()

Sellback trades on the static /auction page: 'Final owner' column on the top-30-sales table rendering '⇄ <buyer> <trade_price>' when traded_to_abbrev is non-null; get_auction() joins to the traded-to team

risk_posture: Semantically correct: no data → no signal → all derived weights collapse to 0, the league wOBA = 0.0, and downstream consumers get a no-op set of weights instead of a 500.

validation_claims.claim: All nine main pages now load on a fresh league: /, /standings, /team/1, /player/1, /teams, /leaders, /players, /youth, /auction → all 200.
validation_claims.check_method: Seed a fresh league and request all nine routes

validation_claims.claim: End-to-end smoke (rng_seed=42, 56-team tiered): all listed pages 200; /team/1 shows manager card + salary column; /player/1 salary line; /auction 'Final owner' column with ⇄ arrows on the 7 trades; /auction/live and /youth unchanged from PR #36; /leaders pre-existing salary leaderboard works.
validation_claims.check_method: Seed rng_seed=42 56-team tiered league and smoke-test routes

non_actions.item: Responsive layout fix for the manager card on narrow viewports
non_actions.reason: Punting on this until the rest of the responsive behaviour gets a pass.

non_actions.item: Splitting audit-fix and bug-fix into separate PRs
non_actions.reason: the bug-fix was discovered while smoke-testing the audit fixes. ... For low-stakes work it's fine; in a more careful environment that's a habit to break.

residual_risks: The fix is correct but it papers over a structural issue: derive_linear_weights mixes 'summarise the existing run-expectancy matrix' with 'derive league wOBA scaling' — one big function with implicit empty-data contracts on dict keys is a footgun.
No fresh-league integration smoke test exists; the failure mode is exactly the one such a test should catch.
When the league has no game data, derive_linear_weights now returns all-zeros — safe but unhelpful (/leaders and /standings show 0.000 wOBA across the board).
Manager card layout is functional but ugly on narrow viewports; mobile wraps the row mid-axis.
open_hooks: Fresh-league smoke test (tests/test_fresh_league.py over each preset config, assert nav routes return 200 before any games are simmed)
derive_linear_weights structural cleanup: split into _empirical_rv_per_event_type() and _woba_scale_factor() (gated on league PA > 0, returns None for empty leagues)
Persisted MLB-fit fallback weights (BB=0.69, HBP=0.72, 1B=0.88, 2B=1.24, 3B=1.56, HR=2.00) until the league has accumulated enough PAs
Manager card on /teams index (archetype label in the team-list row)
Sellback trade column polish: add original-winner abbrev (KCR → ⇄ NYM ƒ47 cr)
Live auction discoverability: nav entry or homepage tile
org_strength tooltip explaining the three things it drives (seed roll bonuses, development rates, auction-day discipline)
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Fresh-League 500 (`derive_linear_weights` KeyError) + Wiring Salary / Manager / Sellback Cards
B. Surfacing data that was silently persisted but never rendered
unmapped_content:
**Branch:** `claude/wire-data-cards`
**Commits (in order):** 3a8092a — Surface salary, manager archetype, and sellback trades in the UI; (fix included in same commit) — derive_linear_weights defensive default for missing event types.

KeyError traceback: KeyError: '1B' at o27v2/analytics/linear_weights.py line 273 in derive_linear_weights. The function had defensive guards on pa > 0 and n_starters > 0, but the dict-key lookups for the raw event types were unguarded. Once games are simmed, the function picks up real values on the next call (_LINEAR_WEIGHTS_CACHE, reset at process boot).

'What I'd do differently' retrospective points: the fresh-league 500 should have been caught by an integration smoke test long ago (function live for weeks, worked on every existing save with real game data); should have run the audit before responding to the user's first prompt rather than after — the Explore agent caught most gaps but produced a few false positives (claimed player flags weren't rendered when they actually were); rule for next time: trust-but-verify on a sub-agent's findings before quoting them — grep takes 5 seconds. Manager axis columns: mgr_quick_hook, mgr_bullpen_aggression, mgr_leverage_aware, mgr_joker_aggression, mgr_pinch_hit_aggression, mgr_platoon_aggression, mgr_run_game, mgr_bench_usage; saber + joker_aggr feed _team_auction_profile.
parser_notes: Clear AAR with a long retrospective section; follow-up pointers mapped to open_hooks.
